text: The grove was not supposed to look like this.
Kale remembered the song his grandmother taught him,
the one that coaxed blossoms from frozen stone.
He hums it now, kneeling at the altar,
pressing his palms flat against the earth.
The ground is cold, not winter-cooled,
The kind of cold that lives in places where nothing will ever grow again.
And it has already begun to bite.
He whispers a word only shamans know.
The silence that follows is worse than any scream.
